title: athyrium filix-femina subsp. jacutense
intro: Athyrium filix-femina subsp. jacutense, also known as Japanese lady fern, is a subspecies of the lady fern plant, native to Japan and parts of Russia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever. The health benefits of Athyrium filix-femina subsp. jacutense include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve respiratory health, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's fronds are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
